contentBlocks.original:
आपदामपहर्तारं दातारं सर्वसम्पदाम् ।
लोकाभिरामं श्रीरामं भूयो भूयो नमाम्यहम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āpadāmapahartāraṃ dātāraṃ sarvasampadām ।
lōkābhirāmaṃ śrīrāmaṃ bhūyō bhūyō namāmyaham ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The remover of all calamities, the giver of all prosperity, the delight of all the worlds — I bow again and again to Śrī Rāma.
contentBlocks.meanings: आपदामपहर्तारं (āpadām-apahartāraṁ) – remover of difficulties (आपदाम्:difficulties | पहर्तारं:remover); दातारं (dātāraṁ) – giver; सर्वसम्पदां (sarvasampadāṁ) – all wealth (सर्व:all | सम्पदां:wealth); लोकाभिरामं (lokābhirāmaṁ) – pleasing to the world (लोक:world | अभिरामं:pleasing); श्रीरामं (śrīrāmaṁ) – Lord Rama; भूयो (bhūyo) – again; भूयो (bhūyo) – again; नमाम्यहम् (namāmyaham) – I bow;

contentBlocks.original:
नमः कोदण्डहस्ताय सन्धीकृतशराय च ।
दण्डिताखिलदैत्याय रामायापन्निवारिणे ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namaḥ kōdaṇḍahastāya sandhīkṛtaśarāya cha ।
daṇḍitākhiladaityāya rāmāyāpannivāriṇē ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the one with bow in hand, with arrow set to the string, the punisher of all demons — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (1)
contentBlocks.meanings: नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; कोदण्डहस्ताय (kodaṇḍahastāya) – to the one with a bow in hand (कोदण्ड:bow | हस्ताय:to the hand); सन्धीकृतशराय (sandhīkṛtaśarāya) – to the one who has prepared the arrow (सन्धीकृत:prepared | शराय:to the arrow); च (ca) – and; दण्डिताखिलदैत्याय (daṇḍitākhiladaityāya) – to the punisher of all demons (दण्डित:punisher | अखिल:all | दैत्याय:to the demons); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, the remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Obeisance to Viṣṇu — to the one solely devoted to protecting the distressed, of boundless splendor — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (2)
contentBlocks.meanings: आपन्नजनरक्षैकदीक्षायामिततेजसे (āpannajanarakṣaikadīkṣāyāmitatejase) – to the one with boundless energy (आपन्नजनरक्षा:protection of distressed people | एक:one | दीक्षा:vow | अमित:boundless | तेजसे:energy); नमोऽस्तु (namo'stu) – obeisance be; विष्णवे (viṣṇave) – to Viṣṇu; तुभ्यं (tubhyaṁ) – to you; रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the Lord of Sītā — in whose presence the sage-women are purified by the dust of his lotus feet — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (3)
contentBlocks.meanings: पदाम्भोजरजस्पर्शपवित्रमुनियोषिते (padāmbhojarajasparśapavitramuniyoṣite) – purified by lotus dust (पदाम्भोज:lotus feet | रजःस्पर्श:dust touch | पवित्र:purified | मुनियोषिते:in the presence of sages); नमोऽस्तु (namo'stu) – obeisance be; सीतापतये (sītāpataye) – to the lord of Sita; रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rama, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rama | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to Raghunātha — who takes the form of the great five-faced lion against the greatly intoxicated elephant of the demon-lord — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (4)
contentBlocks.meanings: दानवेन्द्रमहामत्तगजपञ्चास्यरूपिणे (dānavendramahāmattagajapañcāsyarūpiṇe) – form of powerful demon (दानवेन्द्र:demon lord | महामत्त:greatly intoxicated | गज:elephant | पञ्चास्य:five-faced | रूपिणे:form); नमोऽस्तु (namo'stu) – salutations be; रघुनाथाय (raghunāthāya) – to Raghunatha; रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rama, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rama | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the auspicious form — whose chest is reddened by the kuṅkuma from the bosom of Mahijā (Sītā, the earth-born) — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (5)
contentBlocks.meanings: महिजाकुचसंलग्नकुङ्कुमारुणवक्षसे (mahijākucasaṁlagnakuṅkumāruṇavakṣase) – with chest reddened by kumkuma (महिजा:earth-born | कुच:breast | संलग्न:attached | कुङ्कुमारुण:reddened by kumkuma | वक्षसे:to the chest); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; कल्याणरूपाय (kalyāṇarūpāya) – to the auspicious form (कल्याण:auspicious | रूपाय:to the form); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.original:
पद्मसम्भव भूतेश मुनिसंस्तुतकीर्तये ।
नमो मार्ताण्डवंश्याय रामायापन्निवारिणे ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
padmasambhava bhūtēśa munisaṃstutakīrtayē ।
namō mārtāṇḍavaṃśyāya rāmāyāpannivāriṇē ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the descendant of Mārtāṇḍa (the Sun) — whose fame is praised by Padmasambhava (Brahmā), Bhūtēśa (Śiva), and the sages — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (6)
contentBlocks.meanings: पद्मसम्भवभूतेशमुनिसंस्तुतकीर्तये (padmasambhavabhūteśamunisaṁstutakīrtaye) – praised by sages (पद्मसम्भव:born of lotus | भूतेश:lord of beings | मुनि:sage | संस्तुत:praised | कीर्तये:fame); नमो (namo) – salutations; मार्ताण्डवंश्याय (mārṭāṇḍavaṁśyāya) – descendant of Martanda (मार्ताण्ड:Martanda | वंश्याय:descendant); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – Rama, remover of distress (राम:Rama | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.original:
हरत्यार्तिं च लोकानां यो वा मधुनिषूदनः ।
नमोऽस्तु हरये तुभ्यं रामायापन्निवारिणे ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
haratyārtiṃ cha lōkānāṃ yō vā madhuniṣūdanaḥ ।
namō'stu harayē tubhyaṃ rāmāyāpannivāriṇē ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Obeisance to Hari — who removes the distress of all the worlds, who is Madhunisūdana (slayer of the demon Madhu) — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (7)
contentBlocks.meanings: हरत्यार्तिं (haratyārtim) – removes distress (हरति:removes | आर्तिं:distress); च (ca) – and; लोकानां (lokānāṁ) – of the worlds; यो (yaḥ) – who; वा (vā) – or; मधुनिषूदनः (madhuniṣūdanaḥ) – slayer of Madhu (मधु:Madhu | निषूदनः:slayer); नमोऽस्तु (namo'stu) – obeisance be; हरये (haraye) – to Hari; तुभ्यं (tubhyaṁ) – to you; रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the one known through Vedānta — who appears as the lion against the elephant of worldly suffering — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (8)
contentBlocks.meanings: तापकारणसंसारगजसिंहस्वरूपिणे (tāpakāraṇasaṁsāragajasiṁhasvarūpiṇe) – form of lion to worldly elephant (ताप:suffering | कारण:cause | संसार:worldly existence | गज:elephant | सिंह:lion | स्वरूपिणे:form); नमो (namo) – salutations; वेदान्तवेद्याय (vedāntavedyāya) – known through Vedanta (वेदान्त:Vedanta | वेद्याय:to the one known); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – Rama, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rama | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the form of valor — who bears the arrow that pierces the pride of the rolling ocean — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (9)
contentBlocks.meanings: रङ्गत्तरङ्गजलधिगर्वहृच्छरधारिणे (raṅgattaraṅgajaladhigarvahṛccharadhāriṇe) – bearer of pride's heart arrows (रङ्ग:stage | त्तरङ्ग:waves | जलधि:ocean | गर्व:pride | हृच्छर:heart arrows | धारिणे:bearer); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; प्रतापरूपाय (pratāparūpāya) – to the form of valor (प्रताप:valor | रूपाय:to the form); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the form of truth — who is meditated upon as adorned with the moon, alongside his beloved spouse — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (10)
contentBlocks.meanings: दारोपहितचन्द्रावतंसध्यातस्वमूर्तये (dāropahitacandrāvataṁsadhyātasvamūrtaye) – to the one meditated upon (दारोपहित:adorned with wife | चन्द्रावतंस:moon ornament | ध्यात:meditated upon | स्वमूर्तये:own form); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; सत्यस्वरूपाय (satyasvarūpāya) – to the true form (सत्य:true | स्वरूपाय:to the form); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the slayer of Tāṭakā — of face like the lord of the stars (the moon), of great energy — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (11)
contentBlocks.meanings: तारानायकसङ्काशवदनाय (tārānāyakasaṅkāśavadanāya) – face like moon leader (तारानायक:moon leader | सङ्काश:like | वदनाय:to the face); महौजसे (mahaujase) – of great energy; नमोऽस्तु (namo'stu) – salutations be; ताटकाहन्त्रे (tāṭakāhantre) – slayer of Tāṭakā (ताटक:Tāṭakā | हन्त्रे:slayer); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the one served by Saumitri (Lakṣmaṇa) — who roams the beautiful hermitage of Citrakūṭa's pleasant peaks — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (12)
contentBlocks.meanings: रम्यसानुलसच्चित्रकूटाश्रमविहारिणे (ramyasānulasaccitrakūṭāśramavihāriṇe) – wanderer in beautiful Chitrakuta hermitage (रम्यसानु:beautiful peaks | लसत्:shining | चित्रकूट:Chitrakuta | आश्रम:hermitage | विहारिणे:wanderer); नमस्सौमित्रिसेव्याय (namassaumitrisēvyāya) – salutations to him served by Saumitri (नमः:salutations | सौमित्रि:Saumitri | सेव्याय:served by); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rama, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rama | आपन्नि:distress | वारिणे:remover);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the destroyer of sorrow — devoted to the welfare of all the gods, the destroyer of Daśānana (Rāvaṇa) — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (13)
contentBlocks.meanings: सर्वदेवाहितासक्त (sarvadevāhitāsakta) – devoted to all gods (सर्वदेव:all gods | आहित:devoted | आसक्त:attached); दशाननविनाशिने (daśānanavināśine) – destroyer of Rāvaṇa (दशानन:Rāvaṇa | विनाशिने:destroyer); नमोऽस्तु (namo'stu) – salutations to; दुःखध्वंसाय (duḥkhadhvaṁsāya) – for destruction of sorrow (दुःख:sorrow | ध्वंसाय:for destruction); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the lord of all three worlds — whose lotus feet are the sole worthy dwelling on the jeweled peak — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (14)
contentBlocks.meanings: रत्नसानुनिवासैक (ratnasānunivāsaika) – sole resident of jewel peaks (रत्न:jewel | सानु:peak | निवास:resident | एक:one, sole); वन्द्यपादाम्बुजाय (vandyapādāmbujāya) – to the lotus feet worthy of worship (वन्द्य:worthy of worship | पाद:feet | अम्बुज:lotus); च (ca) – and; नमस्त्रैलोक्यनाथाय (namastrailokyanāthāya) – salutations to the lord of three worlds (नमः:salutations | त्रैलोक्य:three worlds | नाथ:lord); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the remover of impurities — who illumines the bond that is the sole means of liberation from the bondage of worldly existence — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (15)
contentBlocks.meanings: संसारबन्ध (saṁsārabandha) – bondage of worldly life (संसार:worldly life | बन्ध:bondage); मोक्षैकहेतुदामप्रकाशिने (mokṣaikahetudāmaprakāśine) – revealer of the sole cause of liberation (मोक्ष:liberation | एक:sole | हेतु:cause | दाम:bond | प्रकाशिने:revealer); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; कलुषसंहर्त्रे (kaluṣasaṁhartrē) – remover of impurities (कलुष:impurities | संहर्त्रे:remover); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.original:
पवनाशुग सङ्क्षिप्त मारीचादि सुरारये ।
नमो मखपरित्रात्रे रामायापन्निवारिणे ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pavanāśuga saṅkṣipta mārīchādi surārayē ।
namō makhaparitrātrē rāmāyāpannivāriṇē ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the protector of sacrifices — swift as the wind, the one who dispatched Mārīca and other demon-enemies — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (16)
contentBlocks.meanings: पवनाशुगसङ्क्षिप्तमारीचादिसुरारये (pavanāśugasaṅkṣiptamārīcādisurāraye) – enemy of Marica and others (पवनाशुग:swift as wind | सङ्क्षिप्त:compressed | मारीच:Marica | आदि:and others | सुरारये:enemy of gods); नमो (namo) – salutations; मखपरित्रात्रे (makhaparitrātre) – protector of sacrifices (मख:sacrifice | परित्रात्रे:protector); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rama, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rama | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the lotus-eyed — the giver of great bliss to the multitude of genuine, non-hypocritical devotees — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (17)
contentBlocks.meanings: दाम्भिकेतरभक्तौघमहानन्दप्रदायिने (dāmbhiketarabhaktaughamāhānandapradāyine) – giver of great joy to true devotees (दाम्भिकेतर:non-hypocritical | भक्तौघ:devotee multitude | महानन्द:great joy | प्रदायिने:giver); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; कमलनेत्राय (kamalanetrāya) – to the lotus-eyed; रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the cloud-bodied — who severed the head of Kumbhakarṇa, causing terror throughout the three worlds — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (18)
contentBlocks.meanings: लोकत्रयोद्वेगकरकुम्भकर्णशिरश्छिदे (lokatrayodvegakarakumbhakarṇaśiraśchide) – cutter of Kumbhakarna's head (लोकत्रय:three worlds | उद्वेगकर:causing fear | कुम्भकर्ण:Kumbhakarna | शिरश्छिदे:head cutter); नमो (namo) – salutations; नीरददेहाय (nīradadehāya) – to the cloud-bodied; रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rama, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rama | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the one known only to devotees — who bears the playful weapon that stole Kākāsura's single remaining eye — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (19)
contentBlocks.meanings: काकासुरैकनयनहरल्लीलास्त्रधारिणे (kākāsuraikanayanaharallīlāstradhāriṇe) – wielder of playful weapon (काकासुर:Kakasura | एकनयन:one-eyed | हर:remover | लीलास्त्र:playful weapon | धारिणे:wielder); नमो (namo) – salutations; भक्तैकवेद्याय (bhaktaikavedyāya) – known only to devotees (भक्त:devotee | एक:only | वेद्याय:known to); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – Rama, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rama | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the Vāmana form — who, taking the form of a mendicant, strode over and conquered the entire wealth of King Bali — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (20)
contentBlocks.meanings: भिक्षुरूप (bhikṣurūpa) – form of a mendicant (भिक्षु:mendicant | रूप:form); समाक्रान्तबलिसर्वैकसम्पदे (samākrāntabalisarvaikasampade) – possessor of all riches (समाक्रान्त:overcome | बली:Bali | सर्व:all | एक:one | सम्पद:riches); नमो (namo) – salutation; वामनरूपाय (vāmanarūpāya) – to the dwarf form (वामन:dwarf | रूपाय:to the form); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the treasure of liberation — with lotus eyes shimmering with beauty, the radiant-limbed one — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (21)
contentBlocks.meanings: राजीवनेत्रसुस्पन्दरुचिराङ्गसुरोचिषे (rājīvanetrasuspandarucirāṅgasurociṣe) – to the radiant one (राजीवनेत्र:lotus-eyed | सुस्पन्दरुचि:with beautiful splendor | राङ्ग:limbs | सुरोचिषे:radiant); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; कैवल्यनिधये (kaivalyanidhaye) – treasure of liberation (कैवल्य:liberation | निधये:treasure); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the tender-footed one — who dwells amid the Mandāra trees enveloped by gentle breezes — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (22)
contentBlocks.meanings: मन्दमारुतसंवीतमन्दारद्रुमवासिने (mandamārutasaṁvītamandāradrumavāsine) – residing among gentle breezes (मन्दमारुत:gentle breeze | संवीत:enveloped | मन्दारद्रुम:Mandara tree | वासिने:residing); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; पल्लवपादाय (pallavapādāya) – to the tender-footed one (पल्लव:tender | पादाय:to the footed); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the one ever attached to Sītā — whose powerful arms are expert at shattering the bow of Śrīkaṇṭha (Śiva) — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (23)
contentBlocks.meanings: श्रीकण्ठचापदलनधुरीणबलबाहवे (śrīkaṇṭhacāpadalanadhurīṇabalabāhave) – strong enough to break Shiva's bow (श्रीकण्ठ:Shiva | चाप:bow | दलन:breaking | धुरीण:expert | बल:strength | बाहवे:to the arms); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; सीतानुषक्ताय (sītānuṣaktāya) – attached to Sita; रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rama, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rama | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the descendant of Ikṣvāku — the auspicious form worshipped by the queens of kings — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (24)
contentBlocks.meanings: राजराजसुहृद्योषार्चितमङ्गलमूर्तये (rājarājasuhṛdyoṣārcitamangalamūrtaye) – to the auspicious form worshipped by friends of kings (राजराज:king of kings | सुहृद:friend | योषा:woman | अर्चित:worshipped | मङ्गल:auspicious | मूर्ति:form); नम (nama) – salutations; इक्ष्वाकुवंश्याय (ikṣvākúvaṁśyāya) – to the descendant of Ikshvaku (इक्ष्वाकु:Ikshvaku | वंश्य:descendant); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rama, remover of distress (राम:Rama | आपन्न:distress | निवारिण:remover);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the protector of devotees — who alone delights in gazing at his charming reflection in the beautiful mirror — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (25)
contentBlocks.meanings: मञ्जुलादर्शविप्रेक्षणोत्सुकैकविलासिने (mañjulādarśaviprekṣaṇotsukaikavilāsine) – eager for charming reflection (मञ्जुल:charming | आदर्श:mirror | विप्रेक्षण:reflection | उत्सुक:eager | एकविलासिने:unique play); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; पालितभक्त्ताय (pālitabhaktāya) – protector of devotees (पालित:protected | भक्त्ताय:to the devotee); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.original:
भूरिभूधर कोदण्डमूर्ति ध्येयस्वरूपिणे ।
नमोऽस्तु तेजोनिधये रामायापन्निवारिणे ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhūribhūdhara kōdaṇḍamūrti dhyēyasvarūpiṇē ।
namō'stu tējōnidhayē rāmāyāpannivāriṇē ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Obeisance to the treasure of radiance — the great mountain-like bow-bearing form, worthy of meditation — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (26)
contentBlocks.meanings: भूरिभूधरकोदण्डमूर्तिध्येयस्वरूपिणे (bhūribhūdharakodaṇḍamūrtidhyeyasvarūpiṇe) – form meditated upon (भूरि:abundant | भूधर:mountain | कोदण्ड:bow | मूर्ति:form | ध्येय:meditated upon | स्वरूपिणे:to the form); नमोऽस्तु (namo'stu) – obeisance be; तेजोनिधये (tejonidhaye) – to the repository of brilliance (तेजः:brilliance | निधये:to the repository); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्नि:distress | वारिणे:remover);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the great-souled king of kings — the bee intently drinking at the lotus of the yogī-lords' hearts — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (27)
contentBlocks.meanings: योगीन्द्रहृत्सरोजातमधुपाय (yogīndrahṛtsarojātamadhupāya) – to the bee of yogis' hearts (योगीन्द्र:lord of yogis | हृत्सरोज:heart lotus | तमधुपाय:to the bee); महात्मने (mahātmane) – to the great soul; नमो (namo) – salutations; राजाधिराजाय (rājādhirājāya) – to the king of kings (राजा:king | अधिराजाय:to the supreme king); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.original:
भूवराहस्वरूपाय नमो भूरिप्रदायिने ।
नमो हिरण्यगर्भाय रामायापन्निवारिणे ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhūvarāhasvarūpāya namō bhūripradāyinē ।
namō hiraṇyagarbhāya rāmāyāpannivāriṇē ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to Hiraṇyagarbha — who is the Bhū-Varāha form (Earth-Boar), the abundant bestower of all wealth — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (28)
contentBlocks.meanings: भूवराहस्वरूपाय (bhūvarāhasvarūpāya) – to the form of Varaha (भू:earth | वराह:boar | स्वरूपाय:to the form); नमो (namo) – salutations; भूरिप्रदायिने (bhūripradāyine) – to the generous giver (भूरि:abundant | प्रदायिने:to the giver); नमो (namo) – salutations; हिरण्यगर्भाय (hiraṇyagarbhāya) – to Hiranyagarbha; रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rama, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rama | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the treasure of beauty — whose body is adorned with parched rice (lāja) showered from the cupped hands of devoted women — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (29)
contentBlocks.meanings: योषाञ्जलिविनिर्मुक्त (yoṣāñjalivinirmukta) – released from women's hands (योषाञ्जलि:women's hands | विनिर्मुक्त:released); लाजाञ्चितवपुष्मते (lajāñcitavapuṣmate) – adorned with puffed rice (लाज:puffed rice | आञ्चित:adorned | वपुष्मते:having a body); नमस्सौन्दर्यनिधये (namassaudaryanidhaye) – salutations to the beauty treasure (नमः:salutations | सौन्दर्य:beauty | निधये:to the treasure); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्नि:distress | वारिणे:remover);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the Narasiṃha form — whose nails pierced the chest of the lord of demons — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (30)
contentBlocks.meanings: नखकोटिविनिर्भिन्नदैत्याधिपतिवक्षसे (nakhakoṭivinirbhinnadaityādhipativakṣase) – whose chest is pierced by nails (नखकोटि:nail tips | विनिर्भिन्न:pierced | दैत्याधिपति:demon lord | वक्षसे:chest); नमो (namo) – salutations; नृसिंहरूपाय (nṛsiṁharūpāya) – to the form of Narasimha (नृसिंह:Narasimha | रूपाय:form); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Obeisance to the Fish form (Matsya) — who assumed the illusory human body as the cause of the Vedas' rescue — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (31)
contentBlocks.meanings: मायामानुषदेहाय (māyāmānuṣadehāya) – to the illusory human form (माया:illusion | मानुष:human | देहाय:to the body); वेदोद्धरणहेतवे (vedoddharaṇahetave) – cause of Vedas' upliftment (वेद:Vedas | उद्धरण:upliftment | हेतवे:cause); नमोऽस्तु (namo'stu) – obeisance be; मत्स्यरूपाय (matsyarūpāya) – to the fish form (मत्स्य:fish | रूपाय:to the form); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.original:
मितिशून्य महादिव्यमहिम्ने मानितात्मने ।
नमो ब्रह्मस्वरूपाय रामायापन्निवारिणे ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mitiśūnya mahādivyamahimnē mānitātmanē ।
namō brahmasvarūpāya rāmāyāpannivāriṇē ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the honored embodiment of Brahman — the limitless, supremely divine, immeasurable glory — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (32)
contentBlocks.meanings: मितिशून्य (mitiśūnya) – limitless; महादिव्यमहिम्ने (mahādivyamahimne) – to the one with great divine glory (महादिव्य:great divine | महिम्ने:glory); मानितात्मने (mānitātmane) – to the honored self; नमो (namo) – salutations; ब्रह्मस्वरूपाय (brahmasvarūpāya) – to the one embodying Brahman (ब्रह्म:Brahman | स्वरूपाय:to the form); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Obeisance to the embodiment of pure consciousness — who dwells as a welcome guest in the heart-mansions of those free from ego — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (33)
contentBlocks.meanings: अहङ्कारेतरजनस्वान्तसौधविहारिणे (ahaṅkāretarajanasvāntasaudhavihāriṇe) – dweller in others' hearts (अहङ्कार:ego | इतर:other | जन:people | स्वान्त:heart | सौध:mansion | विहारिणे:dweller); नमोऽस्तु (namo'stu) – salutations be; चित्स्वरूपाय (citsvarūpāya) – to the embodiment of consciousness (चित्:consciousness | स्वरूपाय:to the form); रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rāma, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rāma | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the Supreme Soul, the crowned king — who stands resplendent flanked by Sītā and Lakṣmaṇa — to Rāma, the remover of distress. (34)
contentBlocks.meanings: सीतालक्ष्मणसंशोभिपार्श्वाय (sītālakṣmaṇasaṁśobhipārśvāya) – with Sita and Lakshmana by side (सीता:Sita | लक्ष्मण:Lakshmana | संशोभि:adorned | पार्श्वाय:by side); परमात्मने (paramātmane) – to the Supreme Soul; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; पट्टाभिषिक्ताय (paṭṭābhiṣiktāya) – to the crowned one; रामायापन्निवारिणे (rāmāyāpannivāriṇe) – to Rama, remover of distress (रामाय:to Rama | आपन्निवारिणे:remover of distress);

contentBlocks.original:
अग्रतः पृष्ठतश्चैव पार्श्वतश्च महाबलौ ।
आकर्णपूर्णधन्वानौ रक्षेतां रामलक्ष्मणौ ॥ 35 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
agrataḥ pṛṣṭhataśchaiva pārśvataścha mahābalau ।
ākarṇapūrṇadhanvānau rakṣētāṃ rāmalakṣmaṇau ॥ 35 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Before and behind, and on both sides — the two of great might, with bows drawn fully to the ear — may Rāma and Lakṣmaṇa protect me. (35)

contentBlocks.original:
सन्नद्धः कवची खड्गी चापबाणधरो युवा ।
तिष्ठन्ममाग्रतो नित्यं रामः पातु सलक्ष्मणः ॥ 36 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sannaddhaḥ kavachī khaḍgī chāpabāṇadharō yuvā ।
tiṣṭhanmamāgratō nityaṃ rāmaḥ pātu salakṣmaṇaḥ ॥ 36 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Armored, carrying sword, bow and arrows — ever standing before me as a glorious youth — may Rāma protect me, along with Lakṣmaṇa. (36)

contentBlocks.original:
आपदामपहर्तारं दातारं सर्वसम्पदाम् ।
लोकाभिरामं श्रीरामं भूयो भूयो नमाम्यहम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āpadāmapahartāraṃ dātāraṃ sarvasampadām ।
lōkābhirāmaṃ śrīrāmaṃ bhūyō bhūyō namāmyaham ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The remover of all calamities, the giver of all prosperity, the delight of all the worlds — I bow again and again to Śrī Rāma.
contentBlocks.meanings: आपदामपहर्तारं (āpadām-apahartāraṁ) – remover of distress (आपदाम्:of distresses | अपहर्तारं:remover); दातारं (dātāraṁ) – giver; सर्वसम्पदाम् (sarvasampadām) – of all wealth (सर्व:all | सम्पदाम्:of wealth); लोकाभिरामं (lokābhirāmaṁ) – pleasing to the world (लोक:world | अभिरामं:pleasing); श्रीरामं (śrīrāmaṁ) – glorious Rāma (श्री:glorious | रामं:Rāma); भूयो (bhūyo) – again; भूयो (bhūyo) – again; नमाम्यहम् (namāmyaham) – I bow;

contentBlocks.original:
फलश्रुति
इमं स्तवं भगवतः पठेद्यः प्रीतमानसः ।
प्रभाते वा प्रदोषे वा रामस्य परमात्मनः ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
phalaśruti
imaṃ stavaṃ bhagavataḥ paṭhēdyaḥ prītamānasaḥ ।
prabhātē vā pradōṣē vā rāmasya paramātmanaḥ ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Phalaśruti: Whoever recites this hymn to Lord Rāma, the Supreme Soul, with a joyful mind — at dawn or in the evening — (1)

contentBlocks.original:
स तु तीर्त्वा भवाम्बोधिमापदस्सकलानपि ।
रामसायुज्यमाप्नोति देवदेवप्रसादतः ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sa tu tīrtvā bhavāmbōdhimāpadassakalānapi ।
rāmasāyujyamāpnōti dēvadēvaprasādataḥ ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — shall cross the ocean of worldly existence along with all calamities, and attain union with Rāma by the grace of the God of gods. (2)

contentBlocks.original:
कारागृहादिबाधासु सम्प्राप्ते बहुसङ्कटे ।
आपन्निवारकस्तोत्रं पठेद्यस्तु यथाविधिः ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kārāgṛhādibādhāsu samprāptē bahusaṅkaṭē ।
āpannivārakastōtraṃ paṭhēdyastu yathāvidhiḥ ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the afflictions of imprisonment and the like, having fallen into great distress — whoever recites this stotram that removes distress, according to the prescribed method — (3)

contentBlocks.original:
संयोज्यानुष्टुभं मन्त्रमनुश्लोकं स्मरन्विभुम् ।
सप्ताहात्सर्वबाधाभ्यो मुच्यते नात्र संशयः ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saṃyōjyānuṣṭubhaṃ mantramanuślōkaṃ smaranvibhum ।
saptāhātsarvabādhābhyō muchyatē nātra saṃśayaḥ ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — having accompanied it with the Anuṣṭup mantra, remembering the Lord verse by verse — within seven days one is freed from all afflictions; of this there is no doubt. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
द्वात्रिंशद्वारजपतः प्रत्यहं तु दृढव्रतः ।
वैशाखे भानुमालोक्य प्रत्यहं शतसङ्ख्यया ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dvātriṃśadvārajapataḥ pratyahaṃ tu dṛḍhavrataḥ ।
vaiśākhē bhānumālōkya pratyahaṃ śatasaṅkhyayā ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The one of firm vows who recites it thirty-two times each day — and at each sunrise in the month of Vaiśākha, a hundred times — (5)

contentBlocks.original:
धनवान् धनदप्रख्यस्स भवेन्नात्र संशयः ।
बहुनात्र किमुक्तेन यं यं कामयते नरः ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhanavān dhanadaprakhyassa bhavēnnātra saṃśayaḥ ।
bahunātra kimuktēna yaṃ yaṃ kāmayatē naraḥ ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — shall become wealthy, comparable to Kubera the lord of riches; of this there is no doubt. Why say more? Whatever a person desires — (6)

contentBlocks.original:
तं तं काममवाप्नोति स्तोत्रेणानेन मानवः ।
यन्त्रपूजाविधानेन जपहोमादितर्पणैः ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
taṃ taṃ kāmamavāpnōti stōtrēṇānēna mānavaḥ ।
yantrapūjāvidhānēna japahōmāditarpaṇaiḥ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — those desires that person attains through this stotram, also through the rites of yantra worship, japa, homa, and tarpaṇa. (7)

contentBlocks.original:
यस्तु कुर्वीत सहसा सर्वान्कामानवाप्नुयात् ।
इह लोके सुखी भूत्वा परे मुक्तो भविष्यति ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yastu kurvīta sahasā sarvānkāmānavāpnuyāt ।
iha lōkē sukhī bhūtvā parē muktō bhaviṣyati ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever undertakes all this with zeal shall attain all their desires swiftly. Having enjoyed happiness in this world, they shall attain liberation hereafter. (8)
